description: Introduces the basic concepts of Set Theory including: Set-theoretical paradoxes and means of avoiding them, sets, relations, functions, orders and well-orders, proof by transfinite induction and definitions by transfinite recursion, cardinal and ordinal numbers and their arithmetic, construction of the real numbers, the axiom of choice and its consequences.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: (MATH 234and467) or (MATH 341,375,421or521) or graduate/professional standing or member of the Pre-Masters Mathematics (Visiting International) Program
